summary: Refined `struct ftCommonData` by converting many unknown or padding-like fields to `float`, splitting several byte-array gaps into explicit fields, and renaming angle constants with a `_radians` suffix. The renamed radian fields were propagated through common fighter attack, throw, cliff, damage, down/stand, C-stick, and item knockback-angle logic. The PR body says the typing/naming was mostly determined by hex-editor inspection and intended to help automation efforts. The decomp bot reported no GALE01 changes, so these were source/type/name improvements without affecting matched output.

key_files.reason: Main header change: `ftCommonData` field typing, gap splitting, and `_radians` naming.
Updated C-stick aerial angle threshold uses from `x20` to `x20_radians`.
Updated side-tilt angle threshold names including `x9C_radians`, `xA0_radians`, `xA4_radians`, and `xA8_radians`.
Updated side-smash angle threshold names including `xB8_radians`, `xBC_radians`, `xC0_radians`, and `xC4_radians`.
Propagated `x20_radians` to heavy/light item throw direction selection.
Renamed airborne Sakurai-angle fallback field from `x144` to `x144_radians`.
Item knockback angle logic also consumes `p_ftCommonData->x144_radians`.

what_changed: `ftCommonData.x20` became `x20_radians` and all visible uses in L-stick/C-stick angle comparisons were updated.

Additional angle-like fields were renamed with `_radians`: `x9C`, `xA0`, `xA4`, `xA8`, `xB8`, `xBC`, `xC0`, `xC4`, `x144`, plus header-only evidence for fields such as `x1E8_radians`, `x234_radians`, `x238_radians`, `x51C_radians`, and `x560_radians`.

Many `UNK_T` members in `ftCommonData` were retyped to `float`, including ranges around offsets `+108`-`+120`, `+1A4`, `+1B4`, `+1C4`, `+1D4`, `+1D8`, `+2C0`-`+2D0`, `+34C`-`+378`, and others shown in the diff.

Several byte-array placeholder gaps were replaced with explicit fields, e.g. `x84[4]` to `float x84`, `x208[0x210 - 0x208]` to `float x208` and `float x20C`, `x3F0[0x3FC - 0x3F0]` to `x3F0`, `x3F4`, `x3F8`, `x5B8[0x5C0 - 0x5B8]` to `x5B8` and `x5BC`, and `x71C[0x760 - 0x71C]` to explicit fields through `x75C`.
Attack input and motion-decision code now documents that thresholds passed to `ftCo_GetLStickAngle`/`ftCo_GetCStickAngle` are radian values.
`ftCo_Damage_CalcAngle` and item function `it_8027B798` now use `p_ftCommonData->x144_radians` for the airborne 361-degree angle case.
smart_moves: Used value inspection from a hex editor to improve the large data-table struct without attempting risky behavioral rewrites.
Renamed fields only where usage made the unit clear, especially angle thresholds compared directly against stick-angle helper results.
Propagated field renames across all dependent fighter and item callsites in the same PR, keeping the tree buildable and avoiding stale aliases.
Preserved offsets in `ftCommonData` comments while refining types, which is useful for data-table verification and future automation.
Split opaque padding arrays into explicit offset-named fields where individual scalar slots were identified, making future names easier to apply one field at a time.
decomp_lessons: For large common-data structs, unit suffixes such as `_radians` are valuable even before semantic names are known; they prevent degree/radian confusion at callsites.
When a field is repeatedly compared against `ftCo_GetLStickAngle` or `ftCo_GetCStickAngle`, that use pattern is strong evidence for a radian threshold.
Header-only type refinements can still be high-value for automation if they preserve layout and produce no binary changes.
Replacing `UNK_T` or byte-array gaps with scalar fields should be done with offset comments intact so later PRs can audit struct size and alignment.
Shared common-data fields may be consumed outside the fighter system; this PR had to update item code using `p_ftCommonData->x144` as well as fighter damage code.
naming_conventions: Use `_radians` suffix for `ftCommonData` fields known to store angles in radians, even if the semantic field name is still offset-based.
Keep offset-derived placeholder names such as `x20_radians` when the unit is known but the gameplay meaning is not.

Preserve existing semantic names where already known, e.g. `attackhi3_stick_threshold_y`, `kb_min`, `kb_squat_mul`, `x204_knockbackFrameDecay`, `escapeair_deadzone`, `grab_timer_decrement`, and shield-related decay/friction names.
Prefer explicit scalar fields over anonymous byte-array gaps once field type/slot boundaries are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR was effectively a type/name refactor; the decomp-dev bot reported `No changes` for GALE01, indicating matching output was preserved.
Field renames in C callsites do not affect generated code when offsets and types remain layout-compatible.
Changing `UNK_T`/padding to `float` in a data struct can aid decompilation and tooling while remaining nonfunctional if no codegen-sensitive expressions change.
Use cross-file grep-style propagation after renaming shared struct fields; `p_ftCommonData` is referenced in fighter common code and item code.

review_feedback.feedback: Author said the updates were mostly determined by poking around in a hex editor and hoped they would help automation efforts.
GALE01 report: `No changes`, supporting that the struct/type/name update did not alter matched binary output.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `p_ftCommonData->x20` or other unsuffixed angle thresholds after PR 1930.
Audit `ftCommonData` fields still typed as `UNK_T` near offsets where adjacent fields are now known floats.
Find all `deg_to_rad` or `0.0174533f` users that might identify additional `_radians` common-data fields.
Cross-check the explicit fields introduced from byte-array gaps against the source data table to confirm float/int boundaries.
Look for item or physics code using fighter common-data angle fields outside `src/melee/ft`.
